html:
Exclusive to:

Moon Shards are a resource exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them.

There are various ways to obtain Moon Shards. They can be mined from Moon Glass boulders and Glassed Hot Springs in the Lunar Island Biome and can be found naturally on the ground of the Lunar Mine Biome, and also can be mined from Vitreoasis in the Lunar Grotto. Infused Moon Shard would turn into Moon Shard as it spoiled. Lunar Wobster drops one Moon Shard when killed, and Celestial Champion will drop a lot of shards at the end of each phase, and upon its death. Glassed-over Moon Dials will also drop a shard when Celestial Champion dies and the Moonstorms cease.

Hot Springs that have Bath Bombs thrown in will become glassed on a Full Moon and can be mined for Moon Shards, mined Vitreoasis also have a chance to reappear on a Full Moon.

Moon Shards can be used to craft items from the Celestial Tab via the Celestial Altar. They can also be used on a Potter's Wheel to produce glass Sculptures.

Trivia

  • Moon Shards were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
  • Judging by many quotes, these glasses are formed due to water saturated with lunar dirt. In the Scrapbook for Small Vitreoasis it is already specified that this happens only in certain light.
  • Dropping models of Infused Moon Shard actually correspond one-to-one to the regular Moon Shard.

"moonglass"
“It's very sharp.”
–Wilson
“What do we need glass for anyway?”
–Willow
“Is clear green sharp-stuff.”
–Wolfgang
“Its sharp edge would end this quickly.”
–Wendy
“DANGEROUSLY SHARP AND BRIGHT PUKE GREEN. I LOVE IT”
–WX-78
“Perhaps I could keep a sample and study the composition.”
–Wickerbottom
“It's green like a nice leaf.”
–Woodie
“But does it look as sharp as me?”
–Maxwell
“Tis a möst divine substance.”
–Wigfrid
“It came from the moon. Wow!”
–Webber
“My, that's sharp!”
–Warly
“Ouch! Sharp!”
–Wormwood
“Can't wait to try this out.”
–Winona
“In its green sheen I see selene.”
–Wortox
“Glurp! Sharp!”
–Wurt
“Wow... it's so light!”
–Walter
“It would make a perfect watch crystal, if only it played nice with shadow magic.”
–Wanda

There are various ways to obtain Moon Shards. They can be mined from Moon Glass boulders and Glassed Hot Springs in the Lunar Island Biome and can be found naturally on the ground of the Lunar Mine Biome, and also can be mined from Vitreoasis in the Lunar Grotto. Lunar Wobster drops one Moon Shard when killed, and Celestial Champion will drop a lot of shards at the end of each phase, and upon its death. Glassed-over Moon Dials will also drop a shard when Celestial Champion dies and the Moonstorms cease.
Hot Springs that have Bath Bombs thrown in will become glassed on a Full Moon and can be mined for Moon Shards, mined Vitreoasis also have a chance to reappear on a Full Moon.

Judging by many quotes, these glasses are formed due to water saturated with lunar dirt. In the Scrapbook for Small Vitreoasis it is already specified that this happens only in certain light.

There are various ways to obtain Moon Shards. They can be mined from [[Moon Glass]] boulders and [[Hot Spring|Glassed Hot Springs]] in the [[Lunar Island]] [[Biomes|Biome]] and can be found naturally on the ground of the [[Lunar Mine]] [[Biomes|Biome]], and also can be mined from [[Vitreoasis]] in the [[Lunar Grotto]]. [[Infused Moon Shard]] would turn into Moon Shard as it spoiled. [[Lunar Wobster]] drops one Moon Shard when killed, and [[Celestial Champion]] will drop a lot of shards at the end of each phase, and upon its death. Glassed-over [[Moon Dial|Moon Dials]] will also drop a shard when Celestial Champion dies and the [[Moonstorm|Moonstorms]] cease. Hot Springs that have [[Bath Bomb]]s thrown in will become glassed on a [[Moon Cycle|Full Moon]] and can be mined for Moon Shards, mined Vitreoasis also have a chance to reappear on a Full Moon. Moon Shards can be used to craft items from the [[Celestial Tab]] via the [[Celestial Altar]].
